sourceMovie.title: Carrie
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Carrie works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Hereditary
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Carrie clicked for you, Hereditary (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Carrie clicked for you, Gerald's Game (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Carrie?
faq.answer: Try Hereditary, Gerald's Game, The Entity—each shares DNA with Carrie while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Carrie?
faq.answer: Carrie (1976) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Carrie part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Carrie Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
